name: Low-dose metformin improved mouse healthspan and lifespan while high dose was toxic
summary: The strongest classic mammalian aging signal for metformin is dose-sensitive mouse healthspan and lifespan benefit.
statement: In male mice treated from middle age, 0.1% dietary metformin improved physical performance, insulin sensitivity, LDL and cholesterol measures, AMPK activity, antioxidant protection, oxidative-damage accumulation, inflammation, healthspan, and lifespan, while 1% dietary metformin was toxic.
population_or_model: Middle-aged male mouse dietary intervention model
quantitative_note: The PubMed abstract specifies 0.1% w/w dietary metformin as beneficial and 1% w/w as toxic.
caveats: This is mouse evidence, not a human aging trial.
The high-dose toxicity signal makes dose and exposure central to interpretation.
Mouse healthspan and lifespan benefits have not yet translated into confirmed human functional or mortality benefit.
